title: MarginalizingOutThe

lean_statement.name: CDMGNodeIso
lean_statement.code:
-- Helper required to state the row: the cross-carrier graph isomorphism encoding the LN's `\cong`.
/-- A graph isomorphism between two CDMGs over potentially distinct
carrier types, encoded as a carrier *function* (not a carrier
bijection) together with the injectivity-on-nodes condition that
makes it a bijection on the actual node sets.

Concretely, a `CDMGNodeIso G H` packages:

* a function `toFun : α → β`,
* a proof `injOn_nodes` that `toFun` is injective on
  `G.J ∪ G.V` (so, together with the four image-equality fields
  below, it bijects `G`'s nodes onto `H.J ∪ H.V`),
* four image equalities recording that `toFun` sends `G`'s four
  data fields `J / V / E / L` exactly to those of `H`. -/
structure CDMGNodeIso {α β : Type u} (G : CDMG α) (H : CDMG β) where
  /-- The underlying function on carrier types. -/
  toFun : α → β
  /-- The function is injective on `G`'s nodes. -/
  injOn_nodes : Set.InjOn toFun (G.J ∪ G.V)
  /-- The function sends `G.J` to `H.J`. -/
  J_eq : H.J = toFun '' G.J
  /-- The function sends `G.V` to `H.V`. -/
  V_eq : H.V = toFun '' G.V
  /-- The function sends `G.E` to `H.E` (applied componentwise to
  endpoints). -/
  E_eq : H.E = (Prod.map toFun toFun) '' G.E
  /-- The function sends `G.L` to `H.L` (applied componentwise to
  endpoints). -/
  L_eq : H.L = (Prod.map toFun toFun) '' G.L

lean_statement.name: hardInterventionOn_nodeIso_swig_marginalize_outputs
lean_statement.code:
/-- claim_3_19 -- `MarginalizingOutThe`. The hard intervention
`G.hardInterventionOn W` (LHS, over carrier `α`) is
graph-isomorphic to the marginalization of the SWIG over the
`W^o = Sum.inl '' W` copies (RHS, over carrier `α ⊕ ↑W`). The
identification is `split1 W`, which sends `w ∈ W` to
`Sum.inr ⟨w, hw⟩` (the LN's `w ↦ w^i`) and is the identity on
`J ∪ (V ∖ W)` (under the canonical `inl`-embedding, which is the
LN's `v ↦ v^o := v` for `v ∉ W`). -/
noncomputable def hardInterventionOn_nodeIso_swig_marginalize_outputs
    {α : Type u} {G : CDMG α} {W : Set α} (hW : W ⊆ G.V) :
    CDMGNodeIso (G.hardInterventionOn W)
                ((G.swig W hW).marginalize (Sum.inl '' W)) where
  toFun := split1 W
  injOn_nodes := fun _ _ _ _ hxy => split1_injective W hxy
  J_eq := by ...
  V_eq := by ...
  E_eq := by ...
  L_eq := by ...

lean_explanation:
The LN states the graph isomorphism $G_{\doit(W)} \cong (G_{\swig(W)})^{\setminus W^o}$ with witness $w \mapsto w^i$, for any CADMG $G = (J, V, E, L)$ and any subset of output nodes $W \subseteq V$. In Lean: `α : Type u` names the ambient vertex type (left implicit in the LN), `G : CDMG α` is the CADMG, `W : Set α` is the LN's $W$, and `hW : W ⊆ G.V` discharges the LN's $W \subseteq V$ precondition. The hypothesis is load-bearing only on the SWIG side — both `hardInterventionOn` and `marginalize` accept any `W : Set α` regardless of $V$-containment, so it appears in the signature only because `G.swig W hW` demands it.

Two Mathlib idioms do real work. `Set.InjOn f S` is set-restricted injectivity (`f` is injective on `S`), exactly what a node-level bijection needs: injectivity is required only on the actual nodes `G.J ∪ G.V`, not on the whole carrier `α`. The notation `f '' S` is `Set.image f S` (the set `{f(x) | x ∈ S}`). `Prod.map f f`, the canonical componentwise lift of a function to pairs, is how a carrier map acts on the edge sets `E` and `L`, both subsets of `α × α`. The RHS carrier `α ⊕ ↑W` (Lean's `Sum`, with `↑W` the subtype of elements of `W`) follows the convention pinned down in `NodeSplittingOn.lean`: `Sum.inl v` is the observation copy ($v^o$ in the LN) and `Sum.inr ⟨w, hw⟩` is the fresh intervention-input copy ($w^i$). So the LN's $W^o$ is precisely `Sum.inl '' W : Set (α ⊕ ↑W)`.

The statement bundles two declarations. `CDMGNodeIso G H` is a `structure` with one data field (`toFun : α → β`) and five proof fields (a `Set.InjOn` plus four image-equalities for `J / V / E / L`); this captures the LN's $\cong$ between two CDMGs that may live over distinct carriers. `hardInterventionOn_nodeIso_swig_marginalize_outputs` then constructs a `CDMGNodeIso` between `G.hardInterventionOn W` (the LN's $G_{\doit(W)}$) and `(G.swig W hW).marginalize (Sum.inl '' W)` (the LN's $(G_{\swig(W)})^{\setminus W^o}$). The witness carrier map is `split1 W : α → α ⊕ ↑W` (from `NodeSplittingOn.lean`): `v ∈ W` maps to `Sum.inr ⟨v, hv⟩` (the LN's $w \mapsto w^i$), and `v ∉ W` maps to `Sum.inl v` (the identity on $J \cup (V \setminus W)$ under the $v^o = v$ convention).
design_choices:
**The cross-carrier iso is encoded as a function `α → β` plus `Set.InjOn`, not a full carrier bijection `α ≃ β`.** The two CDMGs of this row live over carriers of *different cardinality* — LHS over `α`, RHS over `α ⊕ ↑W`. For any finite `α` and non-empty `W`, no `α ≃ α ⊕ ↑W` exists (the cardinalities differ by $|W|$), so an `Equiv`-shaped statement is literally unprovable in those cases. This is the central obstacle that rules out reusing the same-cardinality `CDMGEquiv` API of claim_3_7 / claim_3_10 (whose carrier bijections rebracket the *same* underlying carrier). The LN's $\cong$ is a *node*-level bijection, not a carrier-level one — it bijects $J \cup V$ on the LHS with $J' \cup V'$ on the RHS and says nothing about the unused parts of the carrier. `Set.InjOn toFun (G.J ∪ G.V)` plus the four image-equalities for `J / V / E / L` captures exactly that, and no more.

**`CDMGNodeIso` is kept local to this file, not promoted to a shared module.** Single-consumer rationale: lifting now would force a placement decision — Section 3.1 alongside `CDMG.lean` (where the four-data-field shape is defined) or Section 3.2 alongside the same-cardinality `CDMGEquiv` — and possibly a unification with `CDMGEquiv`, both better deferred until a *second* cross-carrier-iso row reveals the natural shared API. The same wait-for-second-consumer policy already governs `CDMGEquiv` itself, and the cost of lifting later (re-import, possibly rename) is small compared to locking in the wrong shape now. No `refl` / `symm` / `trans` groupoid laws are written for the same reason: no chaining is yet on the table.

**The carrier map witness is `split1 W`, not a bespoke function.** `split1 W : α → α ⊕ ↑W` (from `NodeSplittingOn.lean`) is exactly the LN's identification by construction: `v ∉ W` maps to `Sum.inl v` (the observation copy, identifying with the original vertex under the $v^o = v$ convention) and `v ∈ W` maps to `Sum.inr ⟨v, hv⟩` (the LN's $w \mapsto w^i$). The LN names only `w ↦ w^i` because the identity on the rest is "implicit by absence of any other rule"; in Lean the implicit rule is `Sum.inl`, which is precisely `split1`'s else-branch. Re-using `split1` removes a degree of freedom (any other equivalent encoding would be definitionally different and force `simp`-rewrites to convert) and aligns this row with the chapter-3 split-graph identifications used by claim_3_6 / claim_3_7 / claim_3_8 / claim_3_12 / def_3_12.

**$W^o$ is rendered as `Sum.inl '' W`, not `Sum.inr '' W` or the raw `W`.** Under the `Sum.inl = w^o` / `Sum.inr = w^i` convention from `NodeSplittingOn.lean`, the LN's $W^o$ — the $o$-copies of $W$ inside the SWIG's carrier $\alpha \oplus \uparrow W$ — is literally `Sum.inl '' W : Set (α ⊕ ↑W)`. Marginalizing `Sum.inr '' W` (the $w^i$ copies) instead would be a *different* and *false* statement: deleting the fresh intervention inputs would collapse the SWIG back toward the observational graph $G$, not toward $G_{\doit(W)}$. Marginalizing the raw `W : Set α` is a type error — `marginalize` expects a `Set (α ⊕ ↑W)`, not a `Set α`. So `Sum.inl '' W` is the unique correct choice.

**The conclusion uses $\cong$ (a `CDMGNodeIso`), not literal `=`.** The two CDMGs live over different carrier types — `CDMG α` versus `CDMG (α ⊕ ↑W)` — so Lean's `=` between them is not even type-correct; an equality statement here would be a category error. The LN's $\cong$ is exactly the graph isomorphism captured by `CDMGNodeIso`. Contrast claim_3_4 / claim_3_8 / claim_3_13, where both sides of the chained equation live over the *same* carrier and the LN writes literal `=` (matched in Lean by an ordinary equality `theorem`).

**`hW : W ⊆ G.V` appears at the signature level, but only because of the SWIG side.** `G.swig W hW` and `G.nodeSplittingOn W hW` carry `hW` as a precondition — load-bearing because without it the fresh split edge `(Sum.inl w, Sum.inr ⟨w, _⟩)` would violate `V_subset` for `w ∈ W ∖ G.V`. By contrast, `hardInterventionOn` and `marginalize` both *dropped* their LN preconditions because their constructions are well-defined for any `W`. So the LHS `G.hardInterventionOn W` does not need `hW`; only the RHS does, which is why the hypothesis appears in the signature.

**The declaration is a `noncomputable def`, not a `theorem`.** The conclusion type `CDMGNodeIso ..` is a `structure` (data, not a `Prop`), so `def` is the correct keyword. The `noncomputable` modifier propagates from the `split1` / `nodeSplittingOn` chain (both `noncomputable` because of the classical case-split on set membership). This matches the declaration style of `nodeSplittingOn_nodeSplittingOn_equiv` and `nodeSplittingOn_comm_equiv` in `TwoDisjointNodeSplittingsCommute.lean`, where a `CDMGEquiv`-valued conclusion is similarly a `noncomputable def`.
